_comment: Verification manifest for PR #403 (Helm chart template reorg). Consumed by scripts/re-verify.sh (a standalone helm/git harness — NOT the skill's Go re-verify.sh, because this PR has no Go code). No production bug was found; all findings are CONTRACT tests that must STAY GREEN. 'all green' == the reorg is still behavior-preserving.

runner.note: No <ref> arg -> resolves the current PR head from .pr. Exit 0 iff every finding is green.

findings.claim: Rendered Helm manifests are byte-for-byte identical between the merge-base and the PR head (after stripping '# Source:' path comments), i.e. the reorg does not add, drop, or alter any deployed object.
findings.check: helm template on both refs, normalize (strip Source comments + blanks, sort docs), diff.

findings.claim: The chart passes 'helm lint' at the PR head.

No stale references to old (pre-reorg) file names remain: old flat helm paths (clusterrole.yaml, manager.yaml, secret-role*, rolebinding.yaml, serviceaccount.yaml, webhook-service*, upgrade/) outside the chart templates dir, AND old config/rbac names (secret_role*, renamed to cert_role* by commit 2885e3b1) anywhere.
findings.check: git grep for old-path patterns (helm + kustomize surfaces).

findings.claim: Every RBAC roleRef target and every ServiceAccount subject in the rendered set resolves to a resource actually defined by the chart (ClusterRoleBinding->ClusterRole rbgs-controller-role; RoleBinding->Role rbgs-controller-secret-role; subjects->ServiceAccount rbgs-controller-sa).
findings.check: parse the PR-head render; assert references subset of defined resources.

findings.claim: kustomize build config/default (the source for `make manifests` -> deploy/kubectl/manifests.yaml, which the PR also touched via config/rbac/secret_role* -> cert_role*) is byte-for-byte identical between merge-base and PR head.
findings.check: kustomize build on both refs, diff. Gracefully SKIPPED (recorded pass) if no kustomize/kubectl is available.
findings.note: Added in review round 1 after commit 2885e3b1 extended the reorg into config/rbac.

liveNote: No live layer. This chart is static YAML; the render-equivalence contract (F1) is the strongest signal — a real deploy of an identical rendered manifest set is by definition unchanged. If desired, `helm upgrade --install` into a scratch namespace and confirm the same 12 objects appear, then delete the namespace.
polarityNote: All findings are contract tests (assert intended state). They PASS now and must STAY GREEN. There are NO bug-canaries, because no bug was found. If any check flips to FAIL on a later push, the reorg regressed (a resource was dropped/altered, lint broke, a stale path crept in, or an RBAC ref dangles).
